last_completed_round.direction_name: Hoist 128x128 Hot-Band Shared Offsets Out Of The Steady-State Loop
last_completed_round.hypothesis: The regular 128x128 sibling already showed that a small register reduction and a cleaner consume path can recover some of the grouped-row loss. The next cheapest win is to hoist warp-local A-row and B-column shared offsets out of the hot-band steady-state loops so the active 128x128 path spends fewer issue slots on invariant pointer math while keeping the same CTA geometry, stage depth, and shared-memory footprint.

notes: Build passed for round 4/10. Node A will measure the result next.
